zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Alexander de Lindsay of Crawford於大約1267年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾時，他的父親David de Lindsay25歲，母親Lady Margaret Lindsay of Lamberton20歲。他和Alice Stewart至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他於1308年在蘇格蘭被列為member of parliament。他去世於大約1314年享年49歲。

nameMeanings.name: Lindsay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Lindsey in Lincolnshire, England. This is first found in the form Lindissi, apparently a derivative of the British name of Lincoln . To this was later added the Old English element ēg ‘island’, since the place was virtually cut off by the surrounding fenland. The surname was taken to Scotland at an early date and is the name of an important and powerful Scottish family.
Irish: adopted as an equivalent ofvarious Gaelic names (see   Lindsey  ).

variants.name: Linzy
meanings: Altered form of Scottish, Irish, or English   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  . Compare   Lenzy  .

variants.name: Linzey
meanings: English: variant of   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  .

variants.name: Lindsey
meanings: Irish: shortened and Anglicized form of various Gaelic surnames, as for example Ó Loingsigh (see Lynch 1), Mac Giolla Fhionntóg (see McClintock ), and Ó Fhloinn (see Flynn and Lynn ).

variants.name: Lincoln
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of Lincoln, so named from an original British name Lindo- ‘lake’ + Latin colonia ‘settlement, colony’. The place was an important administrative center during the Roman occupation of Britain and in the Middle Ages it was a center for the manufacture of cloth, including the famous ‘Lincoln green’.
African American: adoption of the surname in 1 above, in honor of Abraham Lincoln, the 16th president of the US, who abolished slavery.
History: Abraham Lincoln (1809–65), 16th president of the US, was the son of an illiterate laborer, descended from Samuel Lincoln, who had emigrated from England to MA in 1637.

variants.name: Linsey
meanings: Scottish, Irish, or English: variant of   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  .
Americanized form of German Linse or Swiss German Linsi, see Lins .

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Alexander de Lindsay of Crawford was born about 1267, in Ayrshire, Scotland, his father, David de Lindsay, was 25 and his mother, Lady Margaret Lindsay of Lamberton, was 20. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Alice Stewart. In 1308, at the age of 42, his occupation is listed as member of parliament in Scotland. He died about 1314, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Alexander de Lindsay of Crawford est né environ 1267, à Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, David de Lindsay, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret Lindsay of Lamberton, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Alice Stewart. En 1308, à l’âge de 42 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est member of parliament à Écosse. Il est décédé environ 1314, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Alexander de Lindsay of Crawford um 1267 in Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater David de Lindsay 25 und seine Mutter Lady Margaret Lindsay of Lamberton 20. Er hatte mit Alice Stewart mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. 1308, als er 42 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als member of parliament in Scotland aufgeführt. Er starb um 1314 im Alter von 49 Jahren.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Lindsay of Crawfordが約1267年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアで生まれた時，父David de Lindsayは25歳，母Lady Margaret Lindsay of Lambertonは20歳であった。彼はAlice Stewartとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。1308年，42歳の時，スコットランドでの職業はmember of parliamentと記録されている。彼は約1314年に49歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Lindsay of Crawford(이)가 대략 1267년 스컷랜드 에이르실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 David de Lindsay(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Margaret Lindsay of Lamberton(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Alice Stewart(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1308년, 42세의 나이로 그의 직업은 스컷랜드에서 member of parliament이었다. 그는 대략 1314년 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он умер около 1314 г. в возрасте 49 лет.
